observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the acting MT (a) and the Municipal Accountant (MA) to: (i) reconcile the ₱137,451.55 discrepancy between their cash records and make appropriate adjustments in their books; and (ii) conduct reconciliation of general ledger (GL) and cashbook balances at least quarterly to promptly identify and adjust in their books any discrepancies or errors in the recording of collections and deposits; and (b) to continuously comply with the regular deposit of collections received from collecting officers.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA to promptly recognize/adjust in the books the reconciling items identified in the BRSs to reflect the correct balance of the CIB-LCCA account; and (b) acting MT to: (i) coordinate with the depository bank officials to immediately furnish the Municipality with copies of the credit/debit memos necessary to support adjustments; and (ii) notify the payees or holders of checks within one month before checks become stale.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) acting MT to: (i) provide the MAO with a copy of the Certified List of Taxpayers on a timely basis for proper recording of the RPT/SET Receivables; (ii) strengthen collection efforts and implement additional measures to improve collection efficiency and reduce outstanding RPT and SET Receivables balances; and (iii) institute remedies, administrative and/or judicial action, for the collection of over-due taxes as provided under Section 256 of RA No. 7160; and (b) MA to: (i) recognize the RPT and SET receivables at the beginning of the year based on the Certified List of Taxpayers prepared by the MTO; and (ii) coordinate with the MTO to reconcile their records of RPT/SET Receivables and thereafter, conduct regular reconciliation to support the reliability of the accounts.

We recommended that the LCE (a) limit the granting of CAs strictly to what is necessary, justified, and allowed under the above-cited regulation; (b) ensure full liquidation of CAs by strictly enforcing the submission of liquidation reports or refund of the remaining balances to avoid the accumulation of CAs and risk of loss thru theft or misapplication of funds; and (c) require the MA to: (i) issue formal written notices to all concerned AOs with outstanding CAs or when necessary, initiate the withholding of salaries for continued non-compliance with liquidation deadlines; and (ii) conduct regular monitoring and reconciliation of the Advances accounts to ensure timely liquidation in compliance with the applicable provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA to: (i) facilitate the write-off of the unreconciled balances of ₱6,090,400.71 pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006; (ii) prepare the necessary adjustments to reclassify the SEPs from PPE to PPA account; and (iii) derecognize from the books unserviceable PPEs with no future economic benefits or service potential expected; and (b) GSO Officer to: (i) coordinate with the MAO to update subsidiary records and prioritize the completion of the inventory count and reconciliation of PPE items; (ii) immediately request the disposal of unserviceable PPEs to prevent further deterioration and decline in the commercial value of the properties; and (iii) provide the MA with the IIRUP containing the list of unserviceable PPE, as the basis for their derecognition in the books.

We also recommended that the LCE establish an Inventory Committee tasked with conducting a physical inventory of the Municipality’s PPE and ensure that the Committee members are temporarily relieved from their regular duties to complete the undertaking and submit an updated RPCPPE to the MA and AT not later than January 31 of each year.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the concerned departments to exert necessary efforts to locate all the required documents for the titling of the 12 parcels of land acquired through procurement or donation; (b) conduct comprehensive planning to verify ownership and legal status of parcels of land before procurement, which includes checking for existing TCTs, TDs, and any encumbrances or disputes associated with the property; (c) immediately initiate the process of transferring the TCTs to the name of the Municipality upon acquisition; and (d) require the MA and GSO, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to trace the pertinent details of the unreconciled beginning balances and conduct reconciliation between the GL and the Report on Real Properties.

We recommended that the LCE (a) expedite the submission of the formal request to the COA Central Office for the update of the Municipality’s eNGAS; (b) require the MA to: (i) recognize depreciation for all depreciable Infrastructure Assets account; (ii) prepare the JEV for the unrecognized 2017 depreciation on the Power Supply Systems account; (iii) prepare and maintain LRNLCs for all roads and their components; and (iv) provide full and adequate disclosures of the LRN in the NFS as prescribed in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-008; (c) instruct the Municipal Engineer (ME) to: (i) coordinate with the MAO and GSO to assess and evaluate the projects recorded under the Road Networks account; and (ii) provide the latter with the complete and detailed description and cost segregation of the Municipality’s road components indicating specific measurements of the surface type, maintenance costs, initial costs incurred or valuation, date of completion, location, and other necessary data; and (d) require the GSO to coordinate with the ME to complete and submit the Inventory of all Local Roads and Road Map, LRNPCs, and Report on LRN of the Municipality as shown in the Annexes of COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the GSO to: (i) coordinate with the MA to reconcile records and determine the cost of all properties eligible for insurance coverage; and (ii) prepare and maintain an updated inventory of insurable properties to facilitate the timely application for insurance coverage with the GSIS; and (iii) establish and implement an effective internal control system for the monitoring and maintenance of government property records to ensure prompt and complete insurance coverage with the GSIS; (b) require the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to allot a sufficient amount for insurance expenses to cover all insurable properties of the Municipality; (c) remind the accountable personnel of their obligation to regularly monitor and ensure the timely renewal of the LTO registrations of vehicles under their responsibility; and (d) require the MA to: (i) coordinate with the GSO to trace and reconcile MVs insured with GSIS but not reflected in eNGAS; and (ii) enforce accountability by charging penalties to the responsible employees for non-compliance with laws and regulations.

We recommended that the LCE (a) continue ensuring the timely implementation of the NGA-funded PPAs within the targeted timeframe for the promotion of the general welfare of the Municipality’s constituents; to (b) maintain compliance with all required documentary and financial reporting obligations; and (c) ensure that any unutilized funds are promptly returned to the source agencies upon completion of the project.

We recommended that the LCE, as Chairperson of the LSB, direct the LSB Secretary to ensure the timely submission of the quarterly and annual SEF utilization reports using SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 to the DepEd Central Office, SB, and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG, as required under Item 6.1 of JC No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that the LCE, as head of the Municipal Development Council (a) ensure continuous adherence to the 20% NTA allocation to finance the developmental projects of the Municipality; and (b) require the ME to facilitate the implementation of delayed or pending projects in the ensuing year, if warranted.

We recommended that the LCE (a) continuously comply with the LDRRMF rules and regulations, and ensure its optimal utilization to mitigate the loss of lives and properties in the event of natural disasters or calamities; (b) require the MA to: (i) correctly record unexpended balances under TL-LDRRMF, per COA Circular No. 2012-002; and (ii) verify the completeness of supporting documents before processing payments; and (c) require the MDRRMO Officer to submit the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to the AT on or before the 15th day after the end of each month for review and verification.

We recommended that the LCE require the HRMO Head, the MBO, and the MA to: (a) continue strict adherence to existing rules and regulations, as well as the Municipality’s control measures in the engagement, management, and payment of salaries for Casual, JO, and COS personnel; and (b) conduct periodic assessments of the internal controls to ensure their effectiveness and adaptability to any changes.

We recommended that LCE require the GFPS to continue to comply with the proper allocation of the required budget for GAD, preparation of the GPB, timely execution of the GAD PPAs, and submission of the GAD AR.

We recommended that the LCE require the acting MT and MA to: (a) continue to faithfully comply with the provisions of BIR RMC No. 23-2012 on the withholding and remittance of taxes within the prescribed period; and (b) retrieve and reconstruct prior years’ records to reconcile and appropriately address the remaining balance of ₱280,814.72.

We recommended that the LCE require the acting MT and MA to: (a) continue ensuring timely deduction and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations including the government share within the prescribed period; (b) coordinate regularly with GSIS to reconcile employee records and resolve any discrepancies; and (c) retrieve prior years’ records to determine the details of the prior years’ unreconciled balances for appropriate action.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA and acting MT to: (a) continuously comply with the regulations on withholding and remittance of monthly contributions within the prescribed period; and (b) retrieve prior years’ records to determine the details of the prior years’ unreconciled balances for appropriate action.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA and acting MT to: (a) continuously comply with the regulations on the withholding and remittance of monthly contributions within the prescribed period; and (b) retrieve prior years’ records to determine the details of the prior years’ unreconciled balances for appropriate action.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the persons liable to immediately settle the charges totaling to continue practicing fiscal prudence in its operations to reduce, if not completely avoid, future audit suspensions, disallowances, and charges.

(Note: Original text: "We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the persons liable to immediately settle the charges totaling ₱525,119.35.")

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the MT to (a) continuously comply with the deposit of collections daily and intact; and (b) strictly require the collectors concerned to remit to MTO their collections daily and intact or once collections reached ₱5,000.00 to (i) forestall possible loss/misuse of funds; and (ii) provide reliable balances of the CLT and revenue accounts at reporting date. Reiterated in Observation no. 2

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA and MT to exert their best efforts to retrieve prior years’ records to ascertain the details of the reported balance of ₱22,950.00 on bank account that was already closed for appropriate action; (b) MA to promptly recognize/adjust in the books the reconciling items in the BRS to reflect the correct balance of the CIB accounts; and (c) require the MT to request the AGDB officials to immediately furnish the MTO and Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) with copies of the credit/debit memo needed to support the adjustment to be effected in the books.

We recommended that the LCE require the: (a) MA to (i) facilitate write-off of the unreconciled balance of ₱6,224,925.19 in prior years resulting from the set-up of the PPE accounts to eNGAS pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the “One-Time Cleansing of PPE Accounts”; and (ii) prepare the necessary adjustments to reclassify the semi-expendable properties from PPE to appropriate accounts. (b) MT/GSO Chief to (i) obtain the subsidiary records from the MAO, compare the same with their RPCPPE and prioritize the completion of the PPE reconciliation and any discrepancies should be immediately verified and adjusted; (ii) verify and validate the existence, status and whereabouts of reconciling items noted; (iii) maintain updated PCs using the prescribed format as provided under the MNGAS for LGUs; and (iv) conduct physical inventory, ocular inspection and initiate proper disposal proceedings on the remaining unserviceable PPE items.

We recommended that the LCE (a) create a Special Committee that will be tasked to make representations with the LRA and Register of Deeds to uncover the details/information on the recognized parcels of land with no TCTs but with TDs under the name of the Municipality and those supported only with TDs under the name of unknown individuals; and (b) prioritize the registration and transfer of title in the Municipality’s name to ensure ownership over the properties.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA to (i) prepare the adjusting entries to reclassify the value of asphalting of roads recognized as PPE to Repairs and Maintenance Expense; (ii) provide depreciation for all depreciable infrastructure assets for fair presentation of PPE account balances in the books; (iii) maintain Road Network Ledger Cards for each municipal road with complete description/ information based on the report to be submitted by the GSO; and (iv) write a letter of request to COA for the updating of the Municipality’s eNGAS to the latest version; and (b) GSO, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer’s Office, Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) and the Municipal Assessor’s Office, to complete the survey of road networks under the Municipality’s jurisdiction and provide the MA with a complete and detailed description/ information of the assets for proper recognition in the books and provision of their corresponding allowance for depreciation.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Local Finance Committee to include in its budget the insurance expenses of all insurable properties of the Municipality; and (b) MTO/GSO to facilitate the submission of the updated inventory of insurable properties to GSIS so that the necessary insurance coverage can be applied by the GSIS insurance fund.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) MA to (i) coordinate/inquire with the DOH accounting office for the related cost/price of the vaccines received by the Municipality; (ii) recognize in the books the cost of the ending inventory of vaccines as at year-end; and (iii) record future receipts and issuances of vaccines in the accounting books; and (b) MHO to furnish the MAO with monthly reports on the receipt, issuance and balances of vaccines and other pertinent documents relating to the COVID-19 vaccination program.

We recommended that the LCE require the MT and MA to (a) strictly adhere to the latest tax regulations on timely withholding and remittance of taxes; (b) ensure that amounts withheld from the salaries and wages of Municipality’s officials and employees tally with the amounts remitted to the GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth, and in case of discrepancies, make an immediate representation with the concerned agency to address the issue; and (c) retrieve prior years’ records to determine the details of the prior years’ unreconciled balances of the Due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth accounts for appropriate action.

1.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the MT to (a) properly bond all the personnel of the Municipality whose duties and responsibilities permit or require the possession or custody of local funds; and (b) cease the practice of assigning collecting functions to unbonded employees of the MTO.

2.9 We recommended that the LCE require the MT and the Municipal Accountant (MA) to (a) reconcile the difference of ₱86,951.55 between their cash records for appropriate adjustment in their books; and (b) conduct quarterly reconciliation of general ledger (GL) and cashbook balances to promptly identify and adjust in their books any discrepancies or errors in the recording of collections and deposits.

2.10 We also recommended that the LCE require the MT (a) to continuously comply with the regular deposit of collections received from collecting officers; and (b) to strictly require the collectors concerned to remit their collections daily and intact or once collections reached ₱5,000.00 to the MTO to (i) prevent potential loss/misuse of funds; and (ii) provide accurate balances of the CLT and revenue accounts at reporting date.

3.14 We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA to promptly recognize/adjust in the books the reconciling items in the BRSs to reflect the correct balance of the CIB-LCCA account; (b) MT to (i) request the AGDB officials to immediately furnish the Municipality with copies of the credit/debit memos needed to support the adjustment to be effected in the books; and (ii) notify the payees or holders of checks within one month prior to the date when issued checks will become stale; and (c) MA and MT to exert their best efforts to retrieve prior years’ records to ascertain the details of the reported balance of ₱22,950.00 on bank account that was already closed for appropriate action.

4.27 We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA to (i) facilitate the write-off of the unreconciled discrepancy of ₱6,142,802.95 in prior years resulting from the set-up of the PPE accounts to eNGAS pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006; (ii) prepare the necessary adjustments to reclassify the SEP from PPE to PYA account; and (iii) derecognize from the books unserviceable PPE with no future economic benefits or service potential expected; and (b) designated GSO to (i) obtain updated subsidiary records from the MAO, compare the same with their RPCPPE, and prioritize the completion of the PPE reconciliation to identify the details of the discrepancy amounting to ₱17,227,218.32 between the RPCPPE and MAO records, for appropriate action; (ii) immediately request the disposal of unserviceable PPE to prevent further deterioration and decline in commercial value of the properties; and (iii) provide the MA with the IIRUP containing the list of unserviceable PPE, as the basis for their derecognition in the books.

5.16 We recommended that the LCE (a) prioritize the creation of a Special Committee tasked with making representations with the LRA and Register of Deeds to facilitate the transfer of parcels of land supported with Deeds of Donation/Sale and TDs under the name of the Municipality; (b) conduct comprehensive planning to verify ownership and legal status of parcels of land before procurement, which includes checking for existing TCTs, TDs, and any encumbrances or disputes associated with the property; (c) immediately initiate the process of transferring the TCTs to the name of the Municipality upon procurement; and (d) MA and Designated GSO, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to trace the pertinent details of the unreconciled beginning balances and conduct reconciliation between the GL and the Report on Real Properties.

6.16 We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA to (i) provide depreciation for all depreciable Infrastructure Assets account; (ii) prepare the JEV for the depreciation not recognized in CY 2017 for the Power Supply Systems account; (iii) prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Ledger Cards (LRNLCs) for all roads and their components; (iv) provide full and adequate disclosures of the LRN in the NFS as prescribed in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-008; and (v) expedite the request on the updating of the Municipality’s eNGAS; (b) Municipal Engineer (ME) to (i) coordinate with the MAO and GSO to assess and evaluate the projects recorded under the Road Networks account; and (ii) provide the latter with the complete and detailed description and cost segregation of the Municipality’s road components indicating specific measurements of the surface type, maintenance costs, initial costs incurred or valuation, date of completion, location, and other necessary data; and (c) designated GSO to coordinate with the ME to complete and submit the Inventory of all Local Roads and Road Map, LRN Property Cards, and Report on LRN of the Municipality as shown in the Annexes of COA Circular No. 2015-008.

7.9 We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Designated GSO Officer to (i) coordinate with the MA to reconcile records and determine the cost of properties eligible for insurance coverage; and (ii) facilitate the submission of the updated inventory of insurable properties to enable the application of necessary insurance coverage through the GSIS insurance fund; (b) Municipal Budget Officer to allot a sufficient amount for insurance expenses to cover all insurable properties of the Municipality; and (c) MA to coordinate with the GSO to obtain the details of the two units of insured motor vehicles with the GSIS but were not recorded in the eNGAS to facilitate the recording thereof.

8.10 We recommended that the LCE direct the BAC to (a) provide justification in resorting to the alternative method of procurement instead of competitive bidding in the procurement of the reading materials totaling ₱513,994.00; (b) streamline the procurement process by consolidating all procurements under a single BAC to improve oversight and accountability; (c) comply strictly with the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184 regarding procurement of goods through competitive bidding; and (d) ensure that the most advantageous price is obtained by resorting to alternative methods of procurement only in highly exceptional cases and that the same will not constitute splitting of contracts.

9.8 We recommended that the LCE continue to strictly comply with the requirements on the grant of cash advance and submission of liquidation thereof to ICFAU as provided in the COA–DBM–DILG-Governance Commission for GOCCs-DND JC No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

10.7 We recommended that the LCE expedite the implementation the PPAs funded by NGAs within the targeted timeframe for the promotion of the general welfare of the constituents of the Municipality and promptly return the unutilized balances to their respective sources.

11.11 We recommended that the LCE (a) as head of the Municipal Development Council (MDC), continue strict adherence to the allocation of no less than 20% of the NTA to finance the developmental projects of the Municipality; and (b) require the ME to (i) implement the unimplemented projects in the ensuing year, if warranted; and (ii) require the Contractor concerned to immediately repair the cracks observed in the project “Concreting of Road Leading to the Proposed MRF at Barangay Binagbag” to prevent further damages/deterioration at no additional cost to the Municipality.

12.10 We recommended that the LCE require the MA (a) to consistently adhere to the above-mentioned provisions of RA No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 to ensure the availability of DRRMF for the implementation of other disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Municipality; and (b) revert any unutilized amount to the GF after five years to be reallocated to other social services as determined by the local Sanggunian.

13.6 We recommended that the LCE require the MT and MA to continue to faithfully comply with the provisions of BIR RMC No. 23-2012 on the withholding and remittance of taxes within the prescribed period; and (b) retrieve prior years’ records to determine the details of the prior years’ unreconciled balances for appropriate action.

14.5 We recommended that the LCE require the MT and MA to continuously comply with regulations in the deduction and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations including the government share within the prescribed period.

15.5 We recommended that the LCE require the MA and MT to (a) continuously comply with the regulations on withholding and remittance of monthly contributions within the prescribed period; and (b) retrieve prior years’ records to determine the details of the prior years’ unreconciled balances for appropriate action.

16.7 We recommended that the LCE require the MA and MT to (a) continuously comply with the regulations on the withholding and remittance of monthly contributions within the prescribed period; and (b) retrieve prior years’ records to determine the details of the prior years’ unreconciled balances for appropriate action.
17.6 We recommended that the LCE require the persons liable to immediately settle the charges totaling ₱525,119.35.

We recommended that the LCE require the MTO/GSO (a) and MA to regularly reconcile their Inventory accounts’ records to accurately reflect the balances of the Inventory accounts in the books; (b) to conduct actual physical count of inventories on semestral basis and submit the result thereof using the RPCI form; and (c) strictly adhere to the prescribed procedures on the requisition, receipt and issuance of inventories and implement the proper use of the required forms stated in the pertinent provisions of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, specifically on the use of RIS and SSMI.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MPDO and implementing Offices to (i) conduct thorough planning for the developmental PPAs and consider the appropriate timing and phasing of their implementation and completion, to avoid unjustifiable delays; and (ii) maximize the utilization of the 20% DF for projects in order to best serve the Municipality’s constituents; and (b) Municipal Engineer to (i) strictly supervise and monitor the effective and efficient implementation of the PPAs under 20% DF; and (ii) coordinate with the MA of any delay in the completion of the project for the imposition of liquidated damages.
We recommended that the LCE continue to satisfactorily implement the PPAs funded by the NGAs and promptly return the unutilized balances to their respective sources.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the Local Finance Committee to assess the target revenues by examining the actual collections of previous years in the preparation of the budget for succeeding years to come up with a viable and realistic budget; (b) require the departments serving as the collecting arms of the Municipality to improve collection efficiency; and (c) initiate extensive tax collection campaigns, particularly on delinquent accounts for RPT and SET.

We recommended that the LCE (a) instruct the HRMO to (i) install tighter internal control of its payroll funds for casual and JO employees by using the ATM payroll system; and (ii) require the consultant to submit her curriculum vitae together with the list of deliverables/outputs and furnish copy thereof to the AT; and (b) require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to (i) strictly comply with the guidelines for alternative methods of procurement set forth under Annex H of the 2016 RIRR of R.A. No. 9184; and (ii) subject the procurement of consultancy services to public bidding unless the conditions for the use of the alternative mode of procurement are met.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the MDRRMO to (a) refrain from charging against the LDRRMF those PPAs which are not directly related to disaster management and activities; and (b) use the LDRRMF in accordance with Section 5.5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

We recommended that the LCE require the GFPS to (a) continue to comply with the proper allocation of the required budget for GAD, preparation of the GPB, and timely submission of the GAD AR; (b) update the GAD Code in accordance with the guidelines and provide a copy of the updated GAD Code to the AT.
AAR CY 2020
observations.managementAction: The MA did not continuously comply with the timely adjustments of the reconciling items noted in the bank reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: AAR CY 2020
observations.managementAction: The proper use of the RPCI, RIS and RSMI were still not being strictly implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require (a) the
MSWDO Chief and concerned Municipal officials to (i) continue the implementation
in the distribution of emergency subsidy in accordance with the existing guidelines;
and (ii) periodically update the database of the qualified beneficiaries; and (b)
Municipal Accountant (MA) and MT to continue to properly utilize, disburse,
liquidate, record, and report funds received from the National Government.

We recommended that the LCE require the MT to continuously comply with
the policy on remittances of collections to avoid the accumulation of cash in her
custody.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA and the MT to (i) perform
a monthly reconciliation of the Cash in Bank-LCCA account balances appearing in
the Accounting and Treasury records with the Bank records and submit the BRSs to
the AT; (ii) send an official letter of request to Bank officials to furnish the copies of
the credit/debit memos in support of the adjustments to be effected in the books by
the former; and (iii) recognize in the books the noted reconciling items in the BRS to
reflect the correct balance of the CIB-LCCA at year-end; and (b) MT to notify the
payees or holder of checks issued by her Office within one (1) month prior to the date
when issued checks will become stale.

We recommended that the LCE require the MT, acting as GSO, (a) and MA
to regularly reconcile their Inventory accounts’ records and strictly adhere with the
procedures on the requisition, receipt, and issuance of inventories to accurately
reflect the balances of the Inventory accounts in the books of accounts pursuant to
existing rules and regulations; and (b) to conduct actual physical count of inventories
on semestral basis and submit the result thereof using the form RPCI.

We recommended that the LCE:
(a) require the MT, acting as the GSO, to (i) obtain the subsidiary records from
the MAO, compare the same with their RPCPPE and expedite the conduct of physical
inventory of all PPE to avail of the “One-Time” Cleansing of PPE accounts; (ii)
undertake collaborative procedures with the MAO to ensure that all PPEs included
in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in PCs; and (iii) reconcile the results of the physical
inventory and property records with the book inventory;
(b) create a Special Committee that will be tasked to make representations with
the Land Registration Authority (LRA) and Registry of Deeds to uncover the
details/information on the recognized parcels of land with no TCTs but with TDs
under the name of the Municipality and those supported only with TDs under the
name of unknown individuals, for appropriate action;
(c) require the MA (i) to provide depreciation for depreciable road components for
fair presentation of PPE account balances in the books; (ii) to transfer to the books of
accounts the Road Networks carried in the Registry; and (iii) to adjust the
overstatement of the PPE accounts on the unreconciled and unsubstantiated beginning
balances; and
(d) require the ME to assess and evaluate the LRNAs and provide the MA and MT
a complete and detailed description/information of the assets for recognition in the
books and provision of their corresponding allowance for depreciation.

We recommended that the LCE (a) continue to satisfactorily implement the
PPAs funded by NGAs and promptly return the unutilized balances to their
respective Agency sources; and (b) require the ME (i) to strictly supervise and
regularly monitor the implementation of public works projects in accordance with
plans and specifications and the contract time; and (ii) to require the contractor to
repair the damages in certain sections of the completed infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Planning and
Development Officer (MPDO) and implementing Offices to maximize the utilization
of the 20% DF for projects that are in the nature of investment or capital
expenditures in order to best serve the Municipality’s constituents; (b) MPDO to
continue to monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different development
projects of the Municipality; (c) ME to (i) strictly supervise and conduct regular
monitoring of the public works projects of the Municipality for immediate
rectification of defects/damages noted and coordinate with the MA of any delay in
the completion of the project; and (ii) require the immediate repair on the scaling
noted on the Concreting of Kalawakan Road at Barangay Encanto to prevent further
damages/deterioration; and (d) BAC and MA to ensure the completeness of the
documentary requirements for infrastructure projects prior to award of contract.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) Municipal DRRM Officer
(MDRRMO) to monitor the implementation of all LDRRM PPAs to continuously
achieve the desired benefits therefrom; (b) Inspection Officer to ensure completeness
of details indicated on the IAR; (c) MA to (i) require the submission of the deficient
documents; and (ii) require the concerned personnel to ensure that documents are
properly signed and adequately indicate all the necessary information prior to
payment.

We recommended that the LCE require the GFPS to (a) continue to comply
with the proper allocation of the required budget for GAD, preparation of the GPB,
timely submission of the GAD AR, and updating of GAD Code in accordance with
the guidelines; and (b) update the GAD database to serve as a basis in planning and
formulation of GAD-responsive PPAs.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the MENRO to (a) intensify and
involve the barangay governments in monitoring the compliance of their constituents
with environmental laws, rules and ordinances; (b) provide resources for the
establishment of MRFs which would reduce the residual wastes being hauled and
collected by the service provider(s); and (c) continue to properly inform and
encourage the constituents on the proper segregation of waste.

We recommended that the LCE require the MT and MA to continue to comply
with regulations in the withholding and remittance of premiums and loan
amortizations including the government share within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE require the MT and the MA to continue to
comply with regulations on withholding and remittance of premiums and loan
amortizations within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MT and MA to continue to comply
with the regulations on the withholding and remittance of monthly contributions
within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE require the MT and MA to continue to
faithfully comply with BIR RMC No. 23-2012 on the withholding and remittance of
taxes within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE require the persons deemed liable to
immediately settle the disallowances and charges of ₱1,192,419.35.
AAR Calendar Year (CY) 2020
observations.managementAction: The LCE instructed the MT to continuously comply with the deposit of collections regularly and intact.

observations.recommendation: AAR CY 2020
observations.managementAction: The MT still has not reconciled the physical inventory with the book inventory.

observations.recommendation: AAR CY 2020
observations.managementAction: Adhered with the recommendation.

observations.managementAction: Adhered with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: AAR CY 2020
observations.managementAction: Adhered with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: AAR CY 2020
AAR CY 2020

observations.recommendation: AAR CY 2020

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the Municipal Treasurer to exert more efforts in the titling of all lots owned by the Municipality and in the    verification    of       property
without TCTs with the Register of 
Deeds (RD) in order to establish ownership thereto; (b) require the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant  and the Municipal Assessor to (i) reconcile the discrepancies among their records, locate the original copies of titles, prepare the  tax declaration and  the  adjusted RPCPPE; and (ii) provide the acquisition costs or fair market values of the lots, and  their  final  quantity for the fair presentation  of  the Land account in the financial statements;  (c) require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain Property Cards, Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and Real Property Cards for easy reconciliation of the accounts;  and (d)  exhaust all administrative remedies to substantiate the unreconciled beginning balances and if warranted, consider requesting authority from COA to write off unidentified or unsupported beginning balances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Local Finance Committee to include in its budget the insurance expenses of all insurable properties of the Municipality; and (b) Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the submission of the updated inventory of insurable properties to GSIS so that the necessary insurance coverage can be applied by the GSIS insurance fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive commend the Municipal Treasurer in continuously complying with the policy on remittances of collections  and thus, avoided  the accumulation of cash in her custody.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive commend  the Municipal Accountant for  continuously complying  with the timely preparation and adjustments of the reconciling items for fair presentation of the Cash in Bank accounts of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Municipal Treasurer (Acting as GSO) and Municipal Accountant to  strictly follow  the procedures embodied in Sections 185-190, Volume 1  of the GAM for LGUs so that the requisition, receipt, and issuance of inventories will be  accurately reflected in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
(a)	Require the Municipal Treasurer (MT) acting as the General Services Officer (GSO) to (i) obtain the subsidiary records from the Accounting Office and       compare the same with their RPCPPE; (ii) Instruct the GSO and Accounting Office to undertake collaborative procedures to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in Property Cards and PPELCs; and (iii) reconcile the book inventory with the results of the physical inventory and property records.
(b)	Create the Inventory Committee who shall have at least one member each from the Accounting and Property Divisions/Units of the agency.  Authorize the Inventory Committee to (i) be temporarily relieved of all their regular duties to devote their full time in the conduct of the physical inventory taking until the same  is completed; (ii) prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) containing, at the least,   the specific assignments/duties of the Committee members, the cut-off date and a schedule specifying the dates and locations of the inventory taking activities from start up to the targeted completion of the physical inventory; (iii) count all  PPE   items  to be tagged with new property stickers; (iv) exhaust all diligent efforts to establish the non-existing/missing PPEs; and (v) If despite diligent efforts, the non-existing/missing PPEs still could not be established, the LCE shall cause the conduct of an investigation to determine the last known location/office where the missing   PPE items were issued/installed, person accountable, circumstances of the loss, and the persons responsible for the loss, among others.
(c) 	Organize the Disposal and Appraisal Committee and instruct them to take immediate steps to dispose of all unserviceable properties to prevent possible loss   and avoid further deterioration in accordance with the guidelines provided by PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989.
(d) 	Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to (i) locate the original copies of titles, prepare the tax declaration and the adjusted RPCPPE; (ii) provide the acquisition costs or fair market values of the lots, and their final    quantity for the fair presentation  of  the Land account in the financial statements; and (iii) exert more efforts in the titling of all lots owned by the Municipality and in the verification of property without TCTs with the Register of Deeds (RD) in order to establish ownership thereto.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  require the (a) HRMO to   submit the copy of the work schedules of employees who were required to report to duty during the pandemic; (b) Department Heads to submit the schedule of employees required to work during the pandemic stating the dates, purposes and work/services rendered  as directed by the Local Chief Executive; and (c) Municipal Accountant to closely verify the correctness of the payrolls/disbursement, and apply corrections as needed, prior to payment.

We commended the Local Chief Executive  for substantially complying with the requirements of LBC No. 125, the Procurement Law, and GPPB Circular 01-2020 for the budget, utilization, and reporting of the Bayanihan Grant for Cities and Municipalities (BGCM) Fund. However, for the noted documentary requirements, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive  instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit the appropriate and valid documentary requirements and to correct the noted deficiencies in documentation.

We commended the Local Chief Executive together with the authorized    LGU representative, the DSWD Officer and other concerned officials for imposing proper measures in profiling the beneficiaries specifically in times of calamities     such as the: (a) conduct of strict house to house screening in accordance with the requirements of DSWD MC No. 9 series of 2020; (b) proper conduct of distribution of SAP to the qualified beneficiaries; and (c) complying with the required documentation in the granting and liquidation of the cash advance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a)  Implementing Officials to maximize the utilization of the 20% DF for projects that     are in the nature of investment or capital expenditures in order to best serve the Municipality’s constituents; (b)  Municipal Planning and Development Officer  to continue to monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different development projects of the Municipality; (c) Municipal Engineer to (i) comply with  the documentary requirements provided by the DPWH Standard Specifications for Highways, Bridges and Airport  for   infrastructure projects; and (ii) strictly supervise and monitor the public works projects of the Municipality; and (e) Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements for infrastructure projects before payment is made.

We commended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) for substantially    complying with the requirements of LBC No. 124, the Procurement Law, and GPPB Circular 01-2020 for the budget, utilization, and reporting of the DRRM Fund. However, for the noted documentary requirements we recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to require   the submission of the deficient documents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) GAD Focal Point System  (GFPS) to continue to comply with the proper allocation of the  required budget for Gender and Development (GAD), preparation of GAD Plan     and Budget (GPB); timely submission of the GAD accomplishment report (AR),     and updating   of GAD Code in accordance with the guidelines; (b) complete the updating of the GAD database to serve as a basis in planning, programming, and formulating GAD responsive projects, programs, and activities.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer to (a) enforce strictly the sanctions on violators of the ordinances/laws/regulations pertaining to solid waste management; (b) further intensify and involve the barangay governments in monitoring the compliance of their constituents with environmental laws, rules and ordinances;  (c) fully implement solid waste avoidance, volume reduction, and waste minimization measures; (d) provide resources for the establishment of Material Recovery   Facilities which would reduce the residual wastes being hauled and collected by the service provider; and (e) continue to properly inform and encourage the    constituents on the proper segregation of waste.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer  and Municipal Accountant to continue to comply with regulations in the withholding and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations including the government share within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  require the Municipal Treasurer  and Municipal Accountant to continue to comply with regulations on withholding and remittance of premiums and loan amortizations within the prescribed period.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive continue to comply with    the regulations on the withholding and remittance of monthly contributions within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to continue to faithfully comply with BIR RMC No. 23-2012 on the withholding and remittance of taxes within the prescribed period.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the persons    deemed liable to immediately settle the disallowances and charges of P1,236,517.90.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) prepare and submit a written report to the DOE stating their non-compliance to the provisions of the Deployment Plan and the Deed of Donation and their  justifications and actions to be taken thereon for consideration of the DOE; Otherwise, (b) cause the return of the e-trike units to the DOE as stipulated in the Deed of Donation.

We recommended that the Municipal Vice Mayor (a) desist from the  practice of assigning JO employees to offices other than the Office of  the Sanggunian; and (b) recall the assignment of said employees to the Office of  the  Sanggunian.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the Municipal Engineer to (i) ensure that the infrastructure projects conform with the documentary requirements provided by the DPWH Standard Specifications for
 Highways, Bridges and Airport; and (ii) instruct the respective contractors to repair the defective and damaged portion of the road projects pursuant to the one year warranty  period from the date of project completion up to final acceptance or the defects liability period pursuant to Section 62 of the CY 2016 IRR of RA No. 9184; and (b) require the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements for infrastructure projects  before payment is made.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Person or the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO) and the Municipal         Planning                and
 Development Officer (MPDO) to (a) lead in mainstreaming GAD perspectives in LGU policies, plans and programs and in monitoring the effective implementation of the annual GPB, GAD Code, other GAD-related policies and plans; and (b) cause the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database which will serve as basis for planning, formulating GAD responsive projects, programs and activities.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented

There is no database of vulnerable sector / families / individuals.

Reiterated in Observation No. 10

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) continue to be compliant with the provisions of R.A. No. 10121 in the allocation of LDRRMF and institutionalizing the disaster risk reduction and management plan; (b) generate a database of vulnerable sector/families/individuals; and (c) maintain    a      stockpile    of      relief goods.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) (a) involve closely the barangay officials in solid waste management and activate the Solid Waste Management Committees in every barangay for the strict enforcement and continuous implementation of the existing environmental laws especially on waste segregation at source; (b) continue to intensify information campaign on solid waste management and educate the constituents on the necessity of  the construction of MRF in every barangay and establishing programs on  composting, recycling, re-use, and recovery of solid waste generated; (c) consider extending financial assistance, resources, and technology to all of
the ten Barangays which  remained non-compliant to the basic requirements of R.A. No. 9003; and (d) install control measures in regulating wastes collection at source to final disposal to ensure correctness of amounts paid for wastes disposal.

We recommended that Management continue to comply with regulations on withholding and remittance of monthly contributions within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management continue to faithfully comply with BIR RMC No. 23-2012 on the withholding and remittance of taxes within the prescribed period.

We recommended that management (a) instruct the Municipal Treasurer to (i) exert more efforts in the titling of all lots owned by the Municipality and in the verification of properties without TCTs with the Register of Deeds (RD) in order to establish ownership and valid claim thereto; (ii) require the GSO, Municipal Accountant  and the Municipal Assessor to reconcile the discrepancies between their records, locate the original copies of titles, prepare the tax            declaration and the  reconciled RPCPPE; (iii) provide the acquisition cost or fair value of the lots, and  their final quantity for the fair presentation of the Land account in the financial              statements;  (b) disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the  carrying amount of  property, plant and equipment retired from active use and held for disposal so as not to mislead users of the Financial Statements; (c) initiate the proper disposal and recording of unserviceable and fully  depreciated machineries and equipment; and (d) exhaust all administrative and legal remedies to substantiate the unreconciled beginning balances and if warranted, consider requesting an authority from COA to write off unidentified or unsupported beginning balances.

We recommended that Management (a) require the Local Finance Committee to include in its budget the insurance expenses of all insurable properties of the Municipality; and (b) require the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the submission of the updated inventory of insurable properties to GSIS so that the necessary insurance coverage can be applied by the GSIS insurance fund.

2.5	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Local Finance Committee to include in its budget the insurance expenses of all insurable properties of the Municipality; and (b) Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the submission of the updated inventory of insurable properties to GSIS so that the necessary insurance coverage can be applied by the GSIS insurance fund.

3.4	We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive (a) prepare and submit a written report to the DOE stating their non-compliance to the provisions of the Deployment Plan and the Deed of Donation and their  justifications and actions to be taken thereon for consideration of the DOE; Otherwise, (b) cause the return of the e-trike units to the DOE as stipulated in the Deed of Donation.

We recommended that the Municipal Vice Mayor (a) desist from the  practice of assigning JO employees to offices other than the Office of  the Sanggunian; and (b) recall the assignment of said employees to the Office of  the  Sanggunian.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the Municipal Engineer to (i) ensure that the infrastructure projects conform with the documentary requirements provided by the DPWH Standard Specifications for Highways, Bridges and Airport; and (ii) instruct the respective contractors to repair the defective and damaged portion of the road projects pursuant to the one year warranty  period from the date of project completion up to final acceptance or the defects liability period pursuant to Section 62 of the CY 2016 IRR of RA No. 9184; and (b) require the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements for infrastructure projects  before payment is made.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Person or the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO) and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to (a) lead in mainstreaming GAD perspectives in LGU policies, plans and programs and in monitoring the effective implementation of the annual GPB, GAD Code, other GAD-related policies and plans; and (b) cause the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database which will serve as basis for planning, formulating GAD responsive projects, programs and activities.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) continue to be compliant with the provisions of R.A. No. 10121 in the allocation of LDRRMF and institutionalizing the disaster risk reduction and management plan; (b) generate a database of vulnerable sector/families/individuals; and (c) maintain a stockpile of relief goods.

8.15	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) (a) involve closely the barangay officials in solid waste management and activate the Solid Waste Management Committees in every barangay for the strict enforcement and continuous implementation of the existing environmental laws especially on waste segregation at source; (b) continue to intensify information campaign on solid waste management and educate the constituents on the necessity of  the construction of MRF in every barangay and establishing programs on  composting, recycling, re-use, and recovery of solid waste generated; (c) consider extending financial assistance, resources, and technology to all of the ten Barangays which  remained non-compliant to the basic requirements of R.A. No. 9003; and (d) install control measures in regulating wastes collection at source to final disposal to ensure correctness of amounts paid for wastes disposal.

observations.managementAction: The unserviceable properties were reported in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) adhere strictly with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the grant of cash advances and stop the practice of granting advance to DepEd officials and employees; and (b) designate a permanent employee of the Municipality as Special Disbursing Officer to perform disbursing functions for the SEF.

We recommended that Management (a) enter into an agreement in a reputable company that will help them address their solid waste problems; (b) provide resources for the establishment of Material Recovery Facilities which would reduce the residual waste; (c) require the barangays to formulate and implement a No-Littering ordinance at their level; (d) continue to properly inform and encourage the constituents on the proper segregation of waste; and (e) enforce strictly the sanctions on violators of the ordinances/laws/regulations pertaining to solid waste management.
observations.managementAction: The management entered into an agreement with Genetron  International to treat, segregate and dispose the residual waste.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) refrain from using the 70% Mitigation Fund of the LDRRMF for activities which are not related to disaster management and preparedness; and (b) require the LDRRMO, through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC), to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to be certified correct by the Local Accountant and submitted to the COA Auditor of the LGU on or before the 15th day after end of each month.
observations.managementAction: The Solid Waste Management Program was approved last April 27, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to promptly remit to the BIR all taxes withheld for the month within the period mandated by law in order to avoid fines and penalties and to help the national government in increasing revenues in order to finance its various programs and projects.

We recommended that Management require (a) the Municipal Treasurer to finish the  conduct of physical count of all properties and submit thereon the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment; (b) the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to exert more efforts in establishing the details and accuracy of the beginning balances of PPE prior to CY 2007;(c) the Accounting Office to perform a reconciliation of the inventory results with the accounting records; and (d) the Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to maintain  and update  PPE ledger cards and Property Cards on every PPE item, respectively.

We recommended that management require the LDRRM Officer, Municipal Planning and Development Officer, Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to comply strictly with the accounting and reporting guidelines pursuant to the provisions of COA circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that management (a) instruct the MPDO and the Local Development Council (LDC) to abide strictly with the guidelines on the planning and implementation of the projects for  the 20% Development Fund including the provision of realistic budgets; (b) avail of straight contracts for infrastructure projects to minimize labor cost; and (c) advise the Municipal Budget Officer to re-appropriate  in CY 2015 the unexpended balance of the appropriations pertaining to the unimplemented projects/activities.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to promptly remit to the BIR all taxes withheld for the month within the period mandated by law in order to avoid fines and penalties and to help the national government in increasing revenues in order to finance its various programs and projects.
We recommended that management require the persons deemed liable to immediately settle the disallowances and charges of ₱1,561,503.66.
observations.managementAction: The disallowances and charges were not fully settled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require (a) the Municipal Treasurer to finish the  conduct of physical count of all properties and submit thereon the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment; (b) the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to exert more efforts in establishing the details and accuracy of the beginning balances of PPE prior to CY 2007; (c) the Accounting Office to perform a reconciliation of the inventory results with the accounting records; (d) the Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to maintain  and update  PPE ledger cards and Property Cards on every PPE item, respectively; and (e) create an appraisal and disposal committee to facilitate the appraisal and disposal of any unserviceable properties that are included in the books.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) adhere strictly with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the grant of cash advances and stop the practice of granting advance to DepEd officials and employees; and (b) designate a permanent employee of the Municipality as Special Disbursing Officer to perform disbursing functions for the SEF.

We recommended that Management (a) require the Local Finance Committee to include in its budget the insurance expenses of all insurable properties of the Municipality; and (b) require the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the submission of the updated list of inventory of insurable properties with the GSIS so that the necessary insurance coverage can be applied with the GSIS insurance fund.

We recommended that Management (a) enter into an agreement in a reputable company that will help them address their solid waste problems; (b) provide resources for the establishment of Material Recovery Facilities which would reduce the residual waste; (c) require the barangays to formulate and implement a No-Littering ordinance at their level; (d) continue to properly inform and encourage the constituents on the proper segregation of waste; and (e) enforce strictly the sanctions on violators of the ordinances/laws/regulations pertaining to solid waste management.

We recommended that Management (a) refrain from using the 70% Mitigation Fund of the LDRRMF for activities which are not related to disaster management and preparedness; and (b) require the LDRRMO, through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC), to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to be certified correct by the Local Accountant and submitted to the COA Auditor of the LGU on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended that Management instruct the officials in-charge of the development projects to supervise and monitor closely the timely and effective implementation of the development projects under their respective offices.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to promptly remit to the BIR all taxes withheld for the month within the period mandated by law in order to avoid fines and penalties and to help the national government in increasing revenues in order to finance its various programs and projects.
We recommended that Management require the persons deemed liable to immediately settle the disallowances and charges of ₱1,391,503.66.

We recommended that Management continue to comply with the posting of all required notices and reports within the period prescribed by DILG Memorandum Circular No.  2011-134 to enhance transparency in its finances and procurement activities.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer (a) to prepare and submit the RAAF to the Audit Team on a monthly basis; (b) to conduct an actual physical count of accountable forms for the early detection of missing accountable forms and misappropriation of funds; and (c) furnish the Accounting Office the original copy of the RAAF within the prescribed deadline to facilitate the reconciliation of the Municipal Treasury’s records; thus, any arising deficiencies will be immediately remedied at both ends.
observations.managementAction: Management has initiated efforts to implement the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Reports are being prepared but not submitted within the prescribed deadline.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management harmonize the Municipality’s GAD plan with the Philippine Development Plan for Gender-Responsive Development and the guidelines under Joint Circular No. 2012-01 and convene the focal persons on GAD and require them to develop performance or success indicators for each GAD activity.

We recommended that management (a) convene  the Local Solid Waste Management Board for the formulation of the Solid Waste Management Plan (SWMP); (b) provide adequate budget for the execution of the SWMP; and (c) enforce strictly the sanctions on violators of the ordinances/laws/regulations pertaining to solid waste management.
observations.managementAction: Management has formulated their Local Solid Waste Management Plan
